sections.paragraph:
προσηλόω 1 aor. προσήλωσα; pass. pf. ptc. pl. προσηλωμένοι (3 Macc 4:9) (Pla. et al.; ins; Philo, De Prov. Fgm. 2 [Eus., PE 8, 14, 24]; Jos., Ant. 5, 208; Just., A I, 58, 3; Mel., P. 76, 556; 80, 591) nail (fast) τί τινι someth. to someth. (Diod S 4, 47, 5) a bond to the cross Col 2:14 (s. Dibelius, Lohmeyer ad loc.; FDölger, Die Sonne der Gerechtigkeit und d. Schwarze 1918, 129ff.—πρ. σταυρῷ=‘crucify’ Diod S 2, 18, 1; Artem. 2, 56; Jos., Bell. 2, 308; Galen, De Usu Part. II 214, 8 Helmr.). Nail the condemned man fast to the pyre MPol 13:3 (the more general sense ‘fasten’ [Jos., Bell. 5, 232] is excluded by the specific mention of nails). Perh. here in the sense chain fast (cp. Lucian, Prometh. 2 κατάκλειε καὶ προσήλου, Dial. Deor. 1, 1).—DELG s.v. ἧλος. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pl. = plural

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 880
